enrichment.short_description: Yoast SEO is the most widely installed WordPress SEO plugin, giving writers real-time optimization guidance, readability analysis, automatic XML sitemaps, and built-in schema markup directly inside the WordPress editor.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plugin; Premium is EUR 118.80 (about $129) per year per site
enrichment.seo_title: Yoast SEO Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what Yoast SEO offers WordPress users in 2026: free and Premium features, pricing, readability analysis, schema markup and how it compares to rivals.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Yoast SEO is a WordPress plugin that helps writers and site owners optimize on-page content for search engines without needing deep SEO expertise. It was created by Dutch SEO consultant Joost de Valk in 2010 and is developed by Yoast, a company headquartered in Wijchen, Netherlands. The plugin is one of the most downloaded WordPress plugins of all time, with millions of active installations across blogs, publishers, e-commerce stores and agency-managed sites. Yoast SEO ships as a free plugin available through the WordPress.org repository, alongside a paid Premium upgrade priced at EUR 118.80 (roughly $129) per year per website. The free version covers core on-page SEO fundamentals: a traffic-light style content analysis that scores keyphrase usage in the title, meta description, headings and body copy; a readability analysis that flags long sentences, passive voice, missing transition words and dense paragraphs across more than twenty-five supported languages; automatically generated and self-updating XML sitemaps; and built-in structured data (schema.org) markup covering articles, organizations, breadcrumbs, FAQ blocks and how-to blocks so pages are eligible for rich results in Google Search. Yoast SEO Premium adds automation aimed at saving time on larger sites: a redirect manager that lets editors create 301 redirects when URLs change or content is deleted, avoiding broken links and lost rankings; internal linking suggestions that recommend relevant existing pages to link to while a new post or page is being written; AI-generated suggestions for SEO titles and meta descriptions; and the ability to optimize a single piece of content for up to five keyphrases and their synonyms instead of just one. Premium subscribers also get one bundled seat for the Google Docs integration, 24/7 access to Yoast's support team, and the companion Local SEO, News SEO and Video SEO add-on plugins at no extra cost. Yoast SEO works alongside the standard WordPress publishing workflow and major page builders and themes, and it is commonly paired with WooCommerce for product pages. Because pricing is per site, agencies and businesses running many WordPress properties need a Premium license for each domain, which is the most commonly cited downside. Overall, Yoast SEO is best understood as an in-editor SEO coach: it does not replace technical SEO auditing or link-building tools, but it standardizes on-page SEO hygiene (titles, metadata, readability, sitemaps and schema) for non-technical content teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Yoast SEO
paragraphs: Yoast SEO is a WordPress plugin that adds an on-page SEO and readability checker directly to the post and page editor. As a writer types, the plugin scores how well the content targets a chosen keyphrase and flags readability issues, giving green, orange or red indicators that make optimization approachable for non-specialists.

The plugin was launched in 2010 by Dutch SEO consultant Joost de Valk and has grown into one of the most installed plugins in the WordPress ecosystem, used by bloggers, publishers, e-commerce stores and agencies managing client sites. Yoast the company is based in Wijchen, Netherlands.

Yoast SEO is distributed as a free plugin through the official WordPress.org plugin repository, with an optional Premium upgrade that unlocks automation such as redirect management, internal linking suggestions, and AI-assisted title and description writing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The free version covers the fundamentals of on-page SEO: keyphrase and readability analysis, automatically generated XML sitemaps that update whenever content is added or removed, and built-in schema.org structured data for articles, organizations, breadcrumbs, FAQ blocks and how-to blocks so pages can qualify for Google rich results.

Premium adds a redirect manager for handling changed or removed URLs, internal linking suggestions surfaced while writing, AI-generated title and meta description suggestions, and the ability to optimize one page for up to five keyphrases and their synonyms rather than a single target term.

Premium licenses also bundle the Local SEO, News SEO and Video SEO add-ons, one seat for the Google Docs integration, and access to 24/7 support from Yoast's team, which is useful for teams that rely on the plugin as their primary SEO workflow tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Yoast SEO's free plugin has no cost and includes SEO analysis, readability analysis, XML sitemaps and core schema markup, making it usable indefinitely for small sites and hobby blogs.
Yoast SEO Premium costs EUR 118.80 (about $129) per year, billed annually per website. Agencies or businesses that run multiple WordPress sites need a separate Premium subscription for each domain.

There is no usage-based or team pricing tier; the plugin is licensed per site rather than per user, which keeps pricing simple but means the cost scales with the number of websites rather than the number of editors.

features.title: Real-time SEO analysis
features.description: Scores keyphrase usage in the title, meta description, headings and body text with a traffic-light system while content is being written.

features.title: Readability analysis
features.description: Flags long sentences, passive voice, missing transition words and dense paragraphs across more than twenty-five supported languages.

features.title: Automatic XML sitemaps
features.description: Generates and keeps sitemaps up to date automatically whenever pages or posts are added, updated or removed.

features.title: Built-in schema markup
features.description: Adds structured data for articles, organizations, breadcrumbs, FAQ blocks and how-to blocks to help pages earn rich results.

features.title: Redirect manager (Premium)
features.description: Lets editors create 301 redirects when URLs change or pages are deleted, without needing a developer or separate plugin.

features.title: Internal linking suggestions (Premium)
features.description: Recommends relevant existing pages to link to while a new post or page is being drafted.

features.title: AI title and description suggestions (Premium)
features.description: Generates suggested SEO titles and meta descriptions using AI, which editors can accept or edit.

features.title: Multiple keyphrase optimization (Premium)
features.description: Allows a single page to be optimized for up to five keyphrases and their synonyms instead of just one.

pros: Deep integration with the WordPress editor gives instant, actionable feedback while content is being written
The free version already covers core SEO fundamentals most small sites need: sitemaps, schema and content analysis
Large support community, extensive documentation and frequent updates aligned with WordPress core changes
Premium automation, such as redirects, internal linking suggestions and AI snippets, addresses genuine time-saving needs
Trusted at scale, with millions of active installs, so it is well tested against WordPress and search engine changes
cons: Premium is licensed per site, so agencies managing many WordPress properties face a recurring cost per domain
Redirect management, internal linking suggestions and AI snippet generation are all locked behind the paid tier
Advanced SEOs sometimes find the analysis rules too rigid for content that intentionally breaks conventional structure
Only works within WordPress, so it offers no value outside that specific CMS ecosystem

faqs.question: Is Yoast SEO free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. The core Yoast SEO plugin is free and includes content analysis, readability analysis, XML sitemaps and basic schema markup. Premium is an optional paid upgrade.

faqs.question: What does Yoast SEO Premium add over the free version?
faqs.answer: Premium adds a redirect manager, internal linking suggestions, AI-generated titles and descriptions, optimization for up to five keyphrases per page, and 24/7 support, plus bundled Local SEO, News SEO and Video SEO add-ons.

faqs.question: How much does Yoast SEO Premium cost?
faqs.answer: Yoast SEO Premium is EUR 118.80 (about $129) per year, billed per website.

faqs.question: Does Yoast SEO generate XML sitemaps automatically?
faqs.answer: Yes, both the free and Premium versions generate XML sitemaps automatically and keep them updated as content changes.

faqs.question: Does Yoast SEO add schema markup to my pages?
faqs.answer: Yes. It automatically adds structured data for articles, organizations, breadcrumbs, and FAQ and how-to blocks, which can help pages qualify for rich results.

faqs.question: Can Yoast SEO optimize a page for more than one keyword?
faqs.answer: The free version optimizes for one keyphrase per page. Premium allows optimization for up to five keyphrases and their synonyms on a single page.

faqs.question: Does Yoast SEO work with any WordPress theme or page builder?
faqs.answer: Yoast SEO is designed to work broadly across WordPress themes and popular page builders, since it hooks into the standard WordPress editor and content fields rather than a specific theme.

faqs.question: Who develops Yoast SEO?
faqs.answer: Yoast SEO is developed by Yoast, a company founded by Joost de Valk in 2010 and headquartered in Wijchen, Netherlands.

use_cases.title: Blog and content publishing
use_cases.description: Content teams use Yoast SEO to check keyphrase usage and readability before hitting publish, standardizing on-page SEO across many contributors.

use_cases.title: E-commerce product and category pages
use_cases.description: Store owners use Yoast SEO alongside WooCommerce to optimize product titles, descriptions and schema so listings are more discoverable in search.

use_cases.title: Multi-site agency SEO management
use_cases.description: Agencies install Yoast SEO across client WordPress sites to enforce consistent metadata, sitemaps and schema without training every client on SEO basics.

features: Optimization for up to 5 keyphrases per page
Local SEO, News SEO and Video SEO add-ons included

enrichment.short_description: You.com is an AI search and research platform, founded by former Salesforce AI leaders, that grounds chatbot answers in real-time web citations and offers ARI, a multi-source AI research agent for enterprise report generation.
enrichment.seo_title: You.com Review 2026: AI Search, ARI Research Agent and Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn what You.com does in 2026: AI-powered search, model-agnostic chat, the ARI research agent, and how its pricing has shifted toward enterprise API access.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: You.com is an AI search and research company founded in 2020 by Richard Socher (former Chief Scientist at Salesforce) and Bryan McCann, and headquartered in Palo Alto, California. It opened to public beta in November 2021 as a privacy-oriented alternative to conventional search engines, combining a chat-style assistant with live web retrieval so that answers are grounded in citations rather than generated purely from a model's training data. A defining characteristic of You.com is that it is model agnostic: rather than committing to a single underlying large language model, the product lets users route queries to models from providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic and Google, alongside You.com's own proprietary research and retrieval agents. In February 2025, You.com launched ARI (Advanced Research and Insights), a deep-research AI agent aimed at regulated industries and enterprise research teams. ARI is designed to scan hundreds of public and private sources in a single research task, then synthesize a polished report complete with citations, charts and visualizations in a few minutes, a workflow the company positions as faster and broader in source coverage than comparable deep-research agents from other AI labs. This shift toward enterprise research and retrieval has coincided with You.com's business model moving away from a consumer subscription search product. Where the company previously offered a free consumer tier plus a Pro plan around $20 per month with access to multiple AI models and larger context windows, by mid-2026 the consumer-facing pricing page had been retired, reflecting a broader pivot toward usage-based API pricing for developers and enterprise customers building on top of You.com's retrieval and research infrastructure. The company has continued to raise capital to support this enterprise pivot: in September 2025 it closed a $100 million Series C round led by Cox Enterprises at a reported $1.5 billion valuation, giving it unicorn status. For SEO and content teams evaluating You.com as a competitor to Google, Perplexity or ChatGPT search features, the relevant distinctions are its citation-first answer format, its multi-model routing, and its increasing enterprise focus through ARI rather than a purely consumer search replacement.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is You.com
paragraphs: You.com is an AI search and research platform that pairs a chat-style assistant with live web retrieval, so answers are grounded in cited sources rather than generated from a model's training data alone. It was founded in 2020 by Richard Socher and Bryan McCann, both formerly of Salesforce's AI research team, and is headquartered in Palo Alto, California.

The product opened to public beta in late 2021, positioning itself as a privacy-conscious alternative to mainstream search engines. Its defining technical choice is being model agnostic: users and API customers can route requests across different large language models from multiple providers rather than being locked into one.

In 2025, You.com introduced ARI, a deep-research AI agent aimed at professional and regulated-industry research work, marking a shift in the company's focus from consumer search toward enterprise research and retrieval infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: You.com's chat assistant grounds responses in real-time web citations, letting users verify claims against the original sources rather than trusting an ungrounded model output.
Because the platform is model agnostic, users and enterprise customers can select or blend outputs from multiple leading language models rather than being tied to a single vendor's model.

ARI, the company's research agent, can scan a large number of public and private sources for a single research task and assemble a structured report with citations, charts and visualizations, aimed at use cases like market research, due diligence and regulated-industry analysis.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: You.com's pricing has changed significantly over its history. It originally offered a free consumer tier alongside a Pro subscription priced around $20 per month, which unlocked access to additional AI models, file uploads and larger context windows.
By mid-2026 the consumer pricing page had been removed from public view, reflecting the company's pivot away from a seat-priced consumer search product.

Current commercial engagement is centered on usage-based API and enterprise licensing for the retrieval and ARI research capabilities, typically arranged directly with the company rather than through a self-serve public pricing page.

features.title: Citation-grounded chat answers
features.description: Responses are generated with live web retrieval and inline citations so users can verify sources rather than trusting an ungrounded answer.

features.title: Model-agnostic routing
features.description: Lets users and API customers choose or combine outputs from multiple large language model providers instead of one fixed model.

features.title: ARI deep research agent
features.description: Scans a large number of public and private sources for a single research task and produces a structured report with citations and visuals.

features.title: Enterprise and private data grounding
features.description: Supports connecting private enterprise files and data sources so research and answers can incorporate internal documents alongside public web data.

features.title: Interactive charts and visualizations
features.description: ARI-generated reports can include charts and graphics to summarize research findings rather than plain text alone.

features.title: API access for developers
features.description: Exposes retrieval and research capabilities through an API so businesses can build search and research features into their own products.

pros: Citation-first design makes it easier to verify AI-generated claims against original sources
Model-agnostic approach avoids lock-in to a single AI provider's outputs
ARI's multi-source research capability is positioned as faster and broader than some competing deep-research agents
Backed by significant recent funding, suggesting continued investment in the research and retrieval product line
cons: Consumer pricing transparency has decreased, with no public pricing page for individual users by mid-2026
The pivot toward enterprise API and research use cases means casual consumer search users may find the product less clearly positioned
As with any AI search tool, answer quality still depends on the underlying models and the freshness of retrieved sources
Enterprise-focused features like ARI are aimed at business use cases, which may be less relevant for individual, everyday search needs

faqs.question: What is You.com?
faqs.answer: You.com is an AI search and research platform that combines a chat assistant with live web retrieval and citations, plus an enterprise research agent called ARI.

faqs.question: Who founded You.com?
faqs.answer: You.com was founded in 2020 by Richard Socher and Bryan McCann, both former Salesforce AI executives.

faqs.question: What is ARI?
faqs.answer: ARI (Advanced Research and Insights) is You.com's deep-research AI agent, launched in 2025, that scans large numbers of public and private sources to produce cited research reports with charts and visuals.

faqs.question: Is You.com free to use?
faqs.answer: You.com previously offered a free consumer tier and a paid Pro plan, but by mid-2026 its consumer pricing page was no longer public as the company shifted toward enterprise and usage-based API pricing.

faqs.question: Does You.com use its own AI model?
faqs.answer: You.com is model agnostic, meaning it lets users and API customers route requests across multiple large language model providers rather than relying on a single proprietary model.

faqs.question: Where is You.com headquartered?
faqs.answer: You.com is headquartered in Palo Alto, California.

faqs.question: How is You.com funded?
faqs.answer: You.com raised a $100 million Series C round led by Cox Enterprises in September 2025 at a reported $1.5 billion valuation.

use_cases.title: Cited AI search and research
use_cases.description: Users who want AI-generated answers backed by verifiable web citations rather than an ungrounded chatbot response.

use_cases.title: Enterprise deep research reports
use_cases.description: Teams use ARI to produce multi-source research reports for market research, due diligence or regulated-industry analysis in minutes rather than hours.

use_cases.title: Building search and research features via API
use_cases.description: Developers integrate You.com's retrieval and research API into their own products to add grounded AI answers or research capabilities.

features: Access to base AI chat and search
Private data and enterprise file grounding
API access for retrieval and research

enrichment.short_description: YouCanBookMe is an online appointment scheduling tool that turns a calendar into a customizable booking page, handling availability, reminders, payments and team round-robin scheduling.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; Individual plan from $9/month
enrichment.seo_title: YouCanBookMe Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Explore YouCanBookMe in 2026: booking page features, calendar integrations, team scheduling and pricing plans from Free through Team tiers.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: YouCanBookMe is an online scheduling and appointment booking tool that connects to a user's calendar (Google Calendar, Outlook or others) and turns it into a public-facing booking page where clients or colleagues can find and reserve an available meeting time. The company was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Bedford, in Bedfordshire, United Kingdom. YouCanBookMe was later acquired by Capacity, an AI-powered customer support automation platform, which expanded its own scheduling capabilities through the acquisition. The product's core workflow is to sync with a user's existing calendar so that only genuinely free time is exposed to bookers, automatically preventing double-booking. Beyond basic scheduling, YouCanBookMe supports customizable booking pages with branded colors, backgrounds and footers, automated confirmation and reminder emails and SMS messages, and native video conferencing integrations with Zoom, Microsoft Teams and Google Meet. It also supports Stripe payment collection at the time of booking, group and round-robin scheduling for teams, password-protected booking pages, and a Chrome browser extension. Pricing follows a freemium structure across four tiers. The Free plan includes one calendar connection and one booking page with core scheduling features. The Individual plan adds more calendar connections and booking pages plus custom branding and premium support. The Professional plan, aimed at growing businesses, adds automated workflows, unlimited custom form questions, promotional codes, a HubSpot integration, access to Zapier for connecting over a thousand other tools, and analytics. The Team plan, YouCanBookMe's most popular tier for organizations, adds team member management, role-based access control, round-robin scheduling that distributes bookings across a team, centralized billing, unlimited API access and personalized onboarding. Pricing is offered with monthly billing, a 10 percent discount for annual billing, and a 20 percent discount for two-year commitments, alongside a 14-day free trial that requires no credit card. Typical customers include consultants, coaches, recruiters, agencies and sales or customer success teams that need clients or prospects to self-schedule meetings without back-and-forth email threads.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is YouCanBookMe
paragraphs: YouCanBookMe is an online scheduling tool that connects to a user's existing calendar and generates a shareable booking page, letting clients, prospects or colleagues pick an available meeting time without email back-and-forth.

The company was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Bedford, United Kingdom. It was later acquired by Capacity, an AI-powered customer support automation platform, which expanded its own scheduling features through the acquisition.
YouCanBookMe is aimed at individuals, small teams and larger organizations alike, with plans that scale from a single free booking page up to team-wide round-robin scheduling and centralized billing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core of the product is calendar syncing that prevents double-booking by only exposing genuinely free times, combined with a customizable booking page that can reflect a brand's colors, background and footer content.

Automated confirmation and reminder communications go out by email and SMS, and native integrations with Zoom, Microsoft Teams and Google Meet automatically attach video conferencing details to booked meetings.

Team-oriented features include group sessions, round-robin distribution of bookings across team members, role-based access control, and workflow automation for higher-tier plans, alongside Stripe payment collection for paid appointments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: YouCanBookMe offers four tiers: Free (one calendar connection, one booking page), Individual (from $9/month, more connections and branding), Professional (adds automation, HubSpot and Zapier integrations, and analytics), and Team (adds team management, round-robin scheduling and unlimited API access).
All paid plans can be billed monthly, or discounted 10 percent for annual billing and 20 percent for two-year billing, and a 14-day free trial is available without requiring a credit card.

Because pricing rises with the number of calendar connections, booking pages and team members needed, small solo users can often stay on the Free or Individual tier, while agencies and sales teams typically need Professional or Team.

features.title: Calendar sync and conflict prevention
features.description: Connects to Google Calendar, Outlook and other calendars so only genuinely available times are shown to bookers, preventing double-booking.

features.title: Customizable booking pages
features.description: Lets users brand their booking page with custom colors, backgrounds and footer content to match their business identity.

features.title: Automated reminders and confirmations
features.description: Sends customizable email and SMS notifications automatically before and after a booking is made.

features.title: Video conferencing integrations
features.description: Automatically attaches Zoom, Microsoft Teams or Google Meet meeting links to bookings made through the page.

features.title: Stripe payment collection
features.description: Allows businesses to collect payment at the time of booking for paid consultations or appointments.

features.title: Round-robin and group scheduling
features.description: Distributes incoming bookings evenly across a team or allows multiple people to book into a shared group session.

features.title: Automated workflows
features.description: On higher tiers, triggers follow-up actions and communications automatically based on booking events.

features.title: Zapier and HubSpot integrations
features.description: Connects bookings to over a thousand other tools via Zapier, or syncs directly with HubSpot on the Professional plan and above.

pros: Free and Individual tiers make it accessible for solo consultants and freelancers before any real commitment
Strong calendar sync reliably prevents double-booking across multiple connected calendars
Team plan's round-robin scheduling and centralized billing suit growing sales and customer success teams
Native Stripe payment collection removes the need for a separate invoicing step for paid appointments
Zapier and HubSpot integrations extend the tool into existing sales and marketing workflows
cons: Higher-value features like automated workflows, analytics and HubSpot integration require the Professional plan or above
Per-user pricing on the Team plan can add up quickly for larger scheduling teams
Some advanced customization options are only available on higher tiers, which may frustrate Free or Individual users
As with most booking tools, non-technical users may need time to configure availability rules and buffer times correctly

faqs.question: Is YouCanBookMe free?
faqs.answer: Yes, YouCanBookMe offers a free plan with one calendar connection and one booking page. Paid plans start at $9/month for more connections and features.

faqs.question: Does YouCanBookMe prevent double-booking?
faqs.answer: Yes, it syncs with connected calendars in real time so only genuinely available times are shown on the booking page.

faqs.question: Can YouCanBookMe collect payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with Stripe so businesses can collect payment at the time of booking.

faqs.question: Does YouCanBookMe support teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Team plan supports round-robin scheduling, role-based access, team member management and centralized billing.

faqs.question: What video conferencing tools does YouCanBookMe integrate with?
faqs.answer: It natively integrates with Zoom, Microsoft Teams and Google Meet, automatically attaching meeting links to bookings.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, YouCanBookMe offers a 14-day free trial on paid plans with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Who owns YouCanBookMe?
faqs.answer: YouCanBookMe was acquired by Capacity, an AI-powered customer support automation platform, which expanded its scheduling capabilities through the acquisition.

use_cases.title: Solo consultant and coach scheduling
use_cases.description: Independent professionals share a single booking page so clients can self-schedule appointments during available times.

use_cases.title: Sales and customer success team scheduling
use_cases.description: Teams use round-robin scheduling to evenly distribute inbound meeting requests among available reps.

use_cases.title: Paid appointment booking

features: Zoom, Teams and Google Meet integration

pricing_plans.price: Per member, contact for current pricing

enrichment.short_description: Youform is an affordable online form and survey builder positioned as a Typeform alternative, offering unlimited forms and responses on a free plan plus conditional logic, payments and team collaboration on paid tiers.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; Pro from $20/month billed annually ($29/month billed monthly)
enrichment.seo_title: Youform Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Typeform Alternative
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what Youform offers in 2026: unlimited free forms, conditional logic, Stripe payments and pricing across Free, Pro and Business plans.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Youform is an online form and survey builder created in 2023 with the explicit goal of being a fairly priced Typeform alternative that still offers a polished interface and user experience. The company is led by co-founders based in the United States (Pennsylvania) and India (Bangalore), operating as a distributed team rather than from a single traditional headquarters. Youform's core differentiator is its free tier: unlike many competing form builders that cap free-tier response volume, Youform's Free plan offers unlimited forms and unlimited responses at no cost, requiring no credit card to start. The free plan also includes conditional logic and calculations, integrations with Google Sheets, Slack, Zapier and webhooks, file uploads up to 10MB, calendar-booking integrations with Calendly, Cal.com and SavvyCal, and a single user account. Paid tiers unlock features aimed at businesses and teams. The Pro plan, priced at $29 per month billed monthly or $20 per month billed annually ($240 per year), adds custom branding and logo removal, Stripe payment collection within forms, custom domains and fonts, partial submission tracking with refill links so respondents can resume an incomplete form, advanced analytics including drop-off rates, and support for up to three team members. The Business plan, priced at $89 per month billed monthly or $60 per month billed annually ($720 per year), adds identity verification features such as email one-time-password verification and phone verification via SMS (which requires a personal Twilio account), an activity log with CSV export for auditing changes, and five included team members, with additional seats available at $10 per month each. Youform is typically evaluated against Typeform, Google Forms, Tally and Jotform, and its main selling points relative to those competitors are the unlimited free response volume and a modern, minimal editing interface. It suits freelancers, small businesses, indie makers and teams that need survey, feedback, lead-generation or intake forms without paying premium form-builder pricing for basic usage.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Youform
paragraphs: Youform is an online form and survey builder built as a more affordable alternative to Typeform, while aiming to keep a similarly clean and modern editing and response experience.
The product launched in 2023 and is run by a distributed team of co-founders based in the United States and India rather than from a single central headquarters.

Its standout feature is a genuinely unlimited free plan: forms and responses are not capped on the free tier, which differentiates it from many competing form builders that gate response volume behind a paywall.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Even on the free plan, Youform supports conditional logic, calculations, file uploads up to 10MB, and integrations with Google Sheets, Slack, Zapier, webhooks, and calendar-booking tools like Calendly, Cal.com and SavvyCal.

The Pro plan adds business-oriented features: custom branding and logo, Stripe payment collection inside forms, custom domains and fonts, partial submission tracking with refill links, and advanced analytics covering drop-off rates.

The Business plan layers on identity verification (email OTP and SMS phone verification), an activity log with CSV export for compliance and auditing, and support for larger teams with additional seats available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Youform's Free plan costs nothing and includes unlimited forms and responses, making it usable indefinitely for individuals and small projects.
The Pro plan is $29/month billed monthly, or $20/month ($240/year) billed annually, and supports up to three team members plus payment collection and custom branding.

The Business plan is $89/month billed monthly, or $60/month ($720/year) billed annually, includes five team members with additional seats at $10/month each, and adds verification and audit-log capabilities aimed at larger or compliance-conscious teams.

features.title: Unlimited free forms and responses
features.description: The free plan does not cap the number of forms created or responses collected, unlike many competing form builders.

features.title: Conditional logic and calculations
features.description: Lets form creators branch questions based on prior answers and perform calculations within the form, available even on the free plan.

features.title: Stripe payment collection
features.description: Pro and Business plans can collect payments directly within a form using Stripe.

features.title: Partial submission tracking and refill links
features.description: Pro plan and above can save incomplete responses and send respondents a link to resume where they left off.

features.title: Custom branding and domains
features.description: Removes Youform branding and supports custom domains and fonts so forms can match a business's identity.

features.title: Identity verification
features.description: Business plan supports email one-time-password verification and SMS phone verification to confirm respondent identity.

features.title: Advanced analytics
features.description: Pro plan and above include analytics with drop-off rate tracking to identify where respondents abandon a form.

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: Paid plans support multiple team members working on the same forms, with an activity log available on the Business plan.

pros: Free plan offers genuinely unlimited forms and responses, unlike most competitors that cap free-tier volume
Modern, minimal interface comparable to Typeform at a lower price point
Native Stripe payments and calendar-booking integrations reduce the need for separate tools
Partial submission tracking with refill links helps recover otherwise-lost responses
Business plan's verification and audit-log features suit teams with compliance requirements
cons: As a newer company founded in 2023, it has a shorter track record than long-established form builders
SMS phone verification on the Business plan requires connecting a personal Twilio account, adding setup complexity
Advanced features like custom domains, payments and analytics are all locked behind paid tiers
No single, traditional company headquarters may be a consideration for enterprise buyers requiring formal vendor documentation

faqs.question: Is Youform really free for unlimited responses?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes unlimited forms and unlimited responses with no credit card required to start.

faqs.question: How much does Youform Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Pro is $29/month billed monthly, or $20/month ($240/year) if billed annually.

faqs.question: Can Youform collect payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pro and Business plans support collecting payments directly within forms using Stripe.

faqs.question: Does Youform support conditional logic?
faqs.answer: Yes, conditional logic and calculations are included even on the free plan.

faqs.question: What does the Business plan add?
faqs.answer: The Business plan adds email and SMS identity verification, an activity log with CSV export, and five team member seats, with additional seats available for $10/month each.

enrichment.short_description: YouTrack is JetBrains' issue tracking and project management tool, supporting Agile boards, Gantt timelines, time tracking, a knowledge base and AI-assisted features for software and cross-functional teams.
enrichment.starting_price: Free for up to 10 users; paid Cloud plans from about $3.67 per user per month billed annually
enrichment.seo_title: YouTrack Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what JetBrains YouTrack offers in 2026: Agile boards, Gantt timelines, time tracking, AI features, and pricing from a free 10-user plan to paid Cloud tiers.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: YouTrack is an issue tracking and project management tool developed by JetBrains, the software company behind IntelliJ IDEA and other developer tools, founded in 2000 and headquartered in Prague, Czech Republic. YouTrack itself was first released on October 29, 2009, as a keyboard-centric bug and issue tracker, and has since expanded into a broader project management platform used by more than 100,000 companies, spanning software development, DevOps, HR, marketing and other cross-functional teams. YouTrack offers Scrum and Kanban board presets alongside fully customizable boards, an interactive Gantt timeline for scheduling tasks, setting estimates and tracking dependencies, built-in time tracking, and a knowledge base for documentation. It also includes a HelpDesk module and a set of AI-assisted features, including text-to-issue transformation that converts natural-language descriptions into structured issues, writing assistance that checks grammar and spelling, and an AI assistant that can summarize content within tasks, articles and support tickets. On pricing, YouTrack remains free for teams of up to 10 users, with the full feature set available regardless of deployment as either YouTrack Cloud or a self-hosted Server installation. This free tier includes Kanban and Scrum boards, time tracking, the knowledge base, custom fields and REST API access without restriction. For teams larger than 10 users, YouTrack Cloud pricing starts at approximately $3.67 per user per month when billed annually, or a slightly higher rate around $3.50 to $5.40 per user per month depending on billing terms, following a price adjustment that took effect October 1, 2025, marking the first pricing change since the plan's prior structure had been stable for roughly five years. Self-hosted Server licensing is also available for organizations that need to keep issue data on their own infrastructure. YouTrack is commonly compared to Jira, Linear and Asana, and its differentiators are its flexible, highly customizable workflow engine, its keyboard-driven interface inherited from its original design, and JetBrains' broader ecosystem integration with tools like TeamCity, Space and the JetBrains IDEs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is YouTrack
paragraphs: YouTrack is an issue tracking and project management tool built by JetBrains, first released in 2009 as a keyboard-centric bug tracker and since expanded into a general-purpose project management platform.

JetBrains, the company behind YouTrack, was founded in 2000 and is headquartered in Prague, Czech Republic. YouTrack itself is used by more than 100,000 companies across software development, DevOps, HR, marketing and other team functions.
The tool is available both as YouTrack Cloud, a hosted SaaS offering, and as a self-hosted Server installation for organizations that need to keep issue data on their own infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: YouTrack provides Scrum and Kanban board presets as well as fully custom boards, an interactive Gantt timeline for scheduling and dependency tracking, and built-in time tracking for capturing effort against tasks.
A built-in knowledge base and HelpDesk module let teams document processes and manage support requests alongside their issue tracker, rather than needing separate tools.

AI-assisted features include text-to-issue transformation that converts natural-language input into structured tickets, writing assistance for grammar and spelling, and an AI assistant that summarizes content across tasks, articles and tickets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: YouTrack is free for up to 10 users with the full feature set, available on either Cloud or self-hosted Server deployments, including Kanban/Scrum boards, time tracking, the knowledge base and REST API access.

For teams larger than 10 users, YouTrack Cloud pricing starts at roughly $3.67 per user per month on annual billing, following a pricing update effective October 1, 2025, the first change after several years of stable pricing.
Self-hosted Server licenses are also available for organizations that need to run YouTrack on their own infrastructure rather than JetBrains' cloud.

Highly flexible workflow and custom field engine suits teams outside pure software development, including HR and marketing
Combines issue tracking, Gantt planning, knowledge base and HelpDesk in one tool, reducing the need for separate systems
AI-assisted features like text-to-issue and summarization can reduce manual ticket-writing overhead
Backed by JetBrains, a long-established developer tools company with deep integration into its broader ecosystem
cons: Recent price increase (effective October 2025) raises costs for teams above 10 users after years of stable pricing
Keyboard-centric, feature-dense interface can have a steeper learning curve than simpler task tools
Less brand recognition among non-technical teams compared to Jira or Asana
Self-hosted deployment requires more setup and maintenance effort than a pure SaaS competitor

enrichment.short_description: youtube-dl-nas is a small open-source, self-hosted queue server that runs in Docker on a NAS or home server, letting authenticated users submit YouTube download jobs processed by yt-dlp via a web and WebSocket interface.
enrichment.seo_title: youtube-dl-nas: Self-Hosted YouTube Download Queue for Your NAS
enrichment.seo_meta_description: youtube-dl-nas is a free, open-source Docker tool for running a queued, authenticated YouTube download server on a home NAS, built on yt-dlp.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: youtube-dl-nas is a free, open-source, self-hosted project maintained by developer hyeonsangjeon on GitHub. It provides an asynchronous WebSocket-based queue server for downloading YouTube videos to a private network-attached storage (NAS) device or home server, packaged as a ready-to-run Docker container. It is a hobby and community-maintained tool rather than a commercial product; it has no known company, headquarters, funding or employee count behind it, and should not be characterized as a business. The project was created to solve a specific practical problem: running a download tool as an exposed web service on a home NAS without proper access control invites abuse, so youtube-dl-nas requires user authentication (accounts created at container setup time) before accepting download requests. Once authenticated, users submit YouTube URLs, and the server processes them through a queue to prevent overload, with real-time status updates delivered over a WebSocket connection so the requester can watch the download's progress rather than polling a page. The project is built on Python 3 and runs on Debian Linux inside its Docker image. It downloads video at resolutions up to 2160p (4K) or can extract audio only, and it supports batch submissions with resume capability if a download is interrupted. A notable technical detail is that the underlying extraction engine was migrated from the original, now largely unmaintained youtube-dl library to yt-dlp, a more actively maintained fork with better error handling and faster adaptation to changes on YouTube's side; the project also automates daily updates to yt-dlp to keep pace with YouTube's frequent site changes that can otherwise break downloaders. A REST API is also available for programmatic interaction with the queue server, in addition to its web UI. Because it is entirely open source and self-hosted, there is no subscription, license fee or vendor lock-in: users deploy their own Docker container on their own hardware, and the developer's role is limited to maintaining the codebase on GitHub rather than operating a hosted service. Given its scope, it is best suited to hobbyists and home-lab or NAS enthusiasts who already run Docker and want a simple, access-controlled way to queue YouTube downloads for personal archival use, rather than to businesses or teams looking for a commercially supported product.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is youtube-dl-nas
paragraphs: youtube-dl-nas is a free, open-source Docker tool that runs a small, authenticated YouTube download queue server, designed specifically to be deployed on a home NAS or self-hosted server rather than a public cloud service.

It is maintained as a personal, community open-source project by developer hyeonsangjeon on GitHub, with no associated company, funding or employee headcount. It should be understood as a hobby and home-lab tool rather than a commercial SaaS product.

The tool addresses a specific risk of running a bare download script as an exposed web service: without authentication, a public download endpoint on a home network can be abused by anyone who finds it. youtube-dl-nas requires user accounts before accepting jobs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Downloads are processed through an asynchronous queue with real-time status updates delivered over a WebSocket connection, so users can watch progress rather than repeatedly refreshing a page.

The project runs on Python 3 and Debian Linux inside a Docker container, supports resolutions up to 2160p (4K) or audio-only extraction, and supports batch job submission with resume support if a download is interrupted partway through.

It was migrated from the original youtube-dl library to yt-dlp, a more actively maintained fork, and the container automates daily updates to yt-dlp so the tool keeps working as YouTube changes its site. A REST API is also available for programmatic use alongside the web interface.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: youtube-dl-nas is entirely free and open source, distributed via GitHub with no license fee, subscription or paid tier.
Because it is self-hosted, the only cost is whatever hardware and network resources the user's own NAS or server requires to run the Docker container.
There is no vendor to purchase support from; ongoing maintenance depends on the open-source project's community and its maintainer continuing to update the code.

features.title: Authenticated download queue
features.description: Requires user accounts created at container setup, preventing unauthorized use of the exposed download endpoint.

features.title: Asynchronous WebSocket queue server
features.description: Processes download jobs in a queue with real-time progress updates delivered over WebSocket rather than page polling.

features.title: Docker-ready deployment

features.title: yt-dlp powered downloading
features.description: Uses yt-dlp, an actively maintained fork of youtube-dl, for more reliable extraction as YouTube changes its site.

features.title: Automated daily yt-dlp updates
features.description: Automatically updates the underlying yt-dlp engine daily to reduce breakage from YouTube-side changes.

features.title: Up to 4K and audio-only downloads
features.description: Supports downloading video at resolutions up to 2160p or extracting audio only.

features.title: Batch and resume support
features.description: Allows submitting multiple downloads at once and resuming interrupted downloads instead of restarting them.

features.title: REST API access
features.description: Exposes a REST API for programmatic interaction with the download queue alongside the standard web interface.

pros: Completely free and open source with no subscription or licensing cost
Authentication protects the download endpoint from abuse when self-hosted on a home network
Actively kept compatible with YouTube changes via automated daily yt-dlp updates
Docker packaging makes deployment on a NAS or home server straightforward for users already running Docker
cons: Maintained as a personal open-source project, so there is no commercial support or guaranteed uptime of maintenance
Requires comfort with Docker and self-hosting; it is not a hosted, one-click consumer product
Scope is narrow, limited to queued YouTube downloading rather than broader media management
As with any YouTube downloader, functionality can be disrupted by changes YouTube makes to its platform until the tool is updated

faqs.question: Is youtube-dl-nas free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is a free, open-source project with no license fee or subscription.

faqs.question: Do I need Docker to run youtube-dl-nas?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is distributed as a Docker container designed to be deployed on a NAS or home server running Docker.

faqs.question: Does youtube-dl-nas require authentication?
faqs.answer: Yes, user accounts are created at container setup time, and authentication is required before the server accepts download jobs, which prevents abuse of an exposed home-network endpoint.

faqs.question: What download engine does youtube-dl-nas use?
faqs.answer: It uses yt-dlp, an actively maintained fork of the original youtube-dl library, and automates daily updates to keep pace with YouTube changes.

faqs.question: What video quality can youtube-dl-nas download?
faqs.answer: It supports resolutions up to 2160p (4K) as well as audio-only downloads.

faqs.question: Who maintains youtube-dl-nas?
faqs.answer: It is maintained by an individual developer, hyeonsangjeon, as a community open-source project on GitHub, not by a company.

faqs.question: Does youtube-dl-nas have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it provides a REST API for programmatic interaction with the download queue in addition to its web and WebSocket interface.

enrichment.short_description: YugabyteDB is an open-source, PostgreSQL-compatible distributed SQL database built for cloud-native, mission-critical applications, offering horizontal scalability and resilience alongside a managed cloud service called YugabyteDB Aeon.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (open-source Community Edition); YugabyteDB Aeon is usage-based with a free trial
enrichment.seo_title: YugabyteDB Review 2026: Distributed SQL Database Features and Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn what YugabyteDB offers in 2026: PostgreSQL compatibility, distributed SQL architecture, open-source licensing, and YugabyteDB Aeon managed cloud pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: YugabyteDB is an open-source, distributed SQL database designed for cloud-native, mission-critical applications that need horizontal scalability and resilience without sacrificing relational database guarantees. It is developed by Yugabyte, a company founded in February 2016 by ex-Facebook engineers Kannan Muthukkaruppan, Karthik Ranganathan and Mikhail Bautin, and headquartered in Sunnyvale, California. The database is runtime-compatible with PostgreSQL, meaning it supports standard PostgreSQL features such as stored procedures, triggers and extensions, so applications written for PostgreSQL can often run against YugabyteDB with minimal changes while gaining distributed, multi-node scalability. Architecturally, YugabyteDB distributes data automatically across multiple nodes and can span availability zones or regions, providing resilience to node, zone or region-level failures without requiring manual sharding logic in the application layer. It supports both YSQL, a PostgreSQL-compatible relational API, and YCQL, a Cassandra-inspired API, giving teams flexibility depending on their query patterns. YugabyteDB is available under an open-source license (the core database is Apache 2.0 licensed) as a free Community Edition that can be self-managed on any infrastructure, alongside paid enterprise editions and a fully managed cloud database-as-a-service called YugabyteDB Aeon. Aeon offers a free trial period to explore a dedicated cluster before committing, and its production pricing follows a unified consumption-based model using YugabyteDB Credits, where 100 Consumption Units equal 1 Credit worth $1 USD, applied consistently whether deploying in Yugabyte's public cloud regions or a customer's own cloud account. Aeon is offered in tiers: a Standard tier aimed at startups and scale-ups needing seamless scalability or availability-zone-level resilience, and a Professional tier aimed at larger, global businesses that need advanced multi-region capabilities, region-level failure resilience, and compliance with data residency requirements. YugabyteDB is typically evaluated by engineering teams building applications that have outgrown a single-node PostgreSQL or MySQL deployment and need built-in horizontal scaling, multi-region resilience or geo-distribution, without moving away from familiar relational SQL semantics.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is YugabyteDB
paragraphs: YugabyteDB is an open-source, distributed SQL database designed for cloud-native applications that need to scale horizontally across nodes, availability zones or regions while retaining relational database guarantees.
It was created by Yugabyte, a company founded in February 2016 by former Facebook engineers Kannan Muthukkaruppan, Karthik Ranganathan and Mikhail Bautin, headquartered in Sunnyvale, California.

A defining feature is PostgreSQL runtime compatibility: YugabyteDB supports stored procedures, triggers and extensions in the same way PostgreSQL does, so many PostgreSQL-based applications can migrate with limited code changes while gaining distributed scalability.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: YugabyteDB automatically distributes and replicates data across nodes, and can span multiple availability zones or regions, providing resilience to node, zone or region-level failures without manual sharding in the application layer.
It exposes two query APIs: YSQL, which is PostgreSQL-compatible for relational workloads, and YCQL, a Cassandra-inspired API for teams with different query pattern needs.

The database is available as an open-source Community Edition for self-managed deployment, as well as through YugabyteDB Aeon, a fully managed cloud database service that can run in Yugabyte's public cloud or a customer's own cloud account.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The core YugabyteDB database is free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license, allowing unrestricted self-hosted use.
YugabyteDB Aeon, the managed cloud service, offers a free trial to explore a full dedicated cluster before committing to a paid plan.

Aeon's production pricing uses a consumption-based credit model, where 100 Consumption Units equal 1 YugabyteDB Credit worth $1 USD, applied consistently across a Standard tier for startups and scale-ups and a Professional tier for larger businesses needing multi-region and data residency capabilities.

features.title: PostgreSQL compatibility
features.description: Supports PostgreSQL features including stored procedures, triggers and extensions, easing migration from existing PostgreSQL applications.

features.title: Automatic horizontal scaling
features.description: Distributes and shards data automatically across nodes without requiring manual sharding logic in the application.

features.title: Multi-region and multi-zone resilience
features.description: Can span availability zones or regions to withstand node, zone or region-level failures.

features.title: Dual query APIs (YSQL and YCQL)
features.description: Offers a PostgreSQL-compatible relational API (YSQL) alongside a Cassandra-inspired API (YCQL) for different workload types.

features.title: Open-source Community Edition
features.description: Provides a free, self-hostable version of the database under the Apache 2.0 license.

features.title: YugabyteDB Aeon managed service
features.description: Fully managed database-as-a-service that can be deployed in Yugabyte's cloud or the customer's own cloud account.

features.title: Consumption-based cloud pricing
features.description: Aeon pricing uses a unified credit-based consumption model applied consistently across deployment options.

features.title: Bring-your-own-cloud deployment
features.description: Aeon can be deployed into a customer's own cloud account rather than only Yugabyte-hosted infrastructure, supporting data residency needs.

pros: Open-source core with a permissive Apache 2.0 license allows free, unrestricted self-hosted use
PostgreSQL compatibility lowers the migration barrier for teams already using PostgreSQL
Built-in horizontal scaling and multi-region resilience avoid the need for custom application-level sharding
Aeon's bring-your-own-cloud option supports data residency and compliance requirements for global businesses
Founded by engineers with distributed systems experience from Facebook, lending credibility for mission-critical workloads
cons: Distributed SQL databases generally add operational complexity compared to a simple single-node PostgreSQL instance
Consumption-based Aeon pricing can be harder to predict upfront than flat per-seat or per-instance pricing
Full enterprise features and advanced multi-region capabilities are gated behind paid Professional-tier pricing
Teams need to evaluate YCQL versus YSQL carefully, since API choice affects tooling and ecosystem compatibility

faqs.question: Is YugabyteDB open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core YugabyteDB database is open source under the Apache 2.0 license and free to self-host.

faqs.question: Is YugabyteDB compatible with PostgreSQL?
faqs.answer: Yes, YugabyteDB is runtime-compatible with PostgreSQL, supporting stored procedures, triggers and extensions.

faqs.question: What is YugabyteDB Aeon?
faqs.answer: YugabyteDB Aeon is Yugabyte's fully managed cloud database service, offered with a free trial and consumption-based pricing thereafter.

faqs.question: How is YugabyteDB Aeon priced?
faqs.answer: Aeon uses a credit-based consumption model where 100 Consumption Units equal 1 YugabyteDB Credit worth $1 USD, applied across Standard and Professional tiers.

faqs.question: Who founded Yugabyte?
faqs.answer: Yugabyte was founded in February 2016 by former Facebook engineers Kannan Muthukkaruppan, Karthik Ranganathan and Mikhail Bautin.

faqs.question: Can YugabyteDB run across multiple regions?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can distribute data across availability zones or regions to provide resilience to zone or region-level failures.

faqs.question: What query languages does YugabyteDB support?
faqs.answer: It supports YSQL, a PostgreSQL-compatible relational API, and YCQL, a Cassandra-inspired API.

faqs.question: Where is Yugabyte headquartered?
faqs.answer: Yugabyte is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California.

use_cases.title: Scaling beyond single-node PostgreSQL

enrichment.short_description: YunoHost is a free, volunteer-maintained self-hosted server operating system built on Debian GNU/Linux that simplifies installing and administering your own web apps, email, file sync and other services.
enrichment.seo_title: YunoHost Review 2026: Self-Hosted Server OS Features and Setup
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what YunoHost offers in 2026: a free, Debian-based self-hosting operating system with a one-click app catalog, SSO/LDAP, and email and file-sync support.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: YunoHost is a free, open-source operating system built on top of Debian GNU/Linux, designed to simplify the administration of a personal or small-organization server and make self-hosting accessible to people without deep system administration expertise. The project originated in February 2012, when a developer known as Kload began building an admin interface for a friend's server, and it grew from that practical starting point into a broader, community-driven distribution. YunoHost is copylefted libre software maintained entirely by volunteers; it is explicitly not a company or for-profit entity, so it has no headquarters, employee count or funding round to report. The name YunoHost is a play on the internet meme phrase 'Y U NO,' reflecting the project's origins as a community response to the difficulty of self-hosting. Technically, YunoHost provides a webadmin interface and a command-line tool (the yunohost command) for managing a server, alongside an application catalog of pre-packaged software that can be installed in a few clicks rather than manually configured. Example applications available through the catalog include blogging platforms, cloud file storage and sync tools such as Nextcloud, personal websites, RSS readers, email services, and collaborative office suites like OnlyOffice and Collabora that integrate with Nextcloud. A key feature is built-in single sign-on (SSO) and LDAP integration: applications packaged for YunoHost that support user registration can hook into YunoHost's central user directory, so a person who logs into the YunoHost user portal is automatically authenticated across all connected apps rather than needing separate credentials for each one. YunoHost also handles general domain, website and email management tasks, such as configuring DNS, TLS certificates and mail delivery, which are traditionally some of the most error-prone parts of running a personal server. Because it is entirely free and open source, there is no pricing tier or subscription; the only cost to a user is the hardware or hosting they choose to run YunoHost on, such as a Raspberry Pi, an old computer or a rented virtual private server. YunoHost is best suited to privacy-conscious individuals, digital rights advocates and small communities who want to run their own services (file sync, email, blogs, chat) instead of relying on third-party SaaS platforms, and who are comfortable with basic server administration even though YunoHost substantially lowers that bar compared to manual Linux server setup.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is YunoHost
paragraphs: YunoHost is a free, open-source operating system built on Debian GNU/Linux that simplifies the administration of a personal or small-organization server, aiming to bring self-hosting to people without deep system administration experience.

The project began in February 2012 when a developer known as Kload started building an admin interface for a friend's server, and it has since grown into a community-maintained distribution used by self-hosters worldwide.
YunoHost is copylefted libre software maintained entirely by volunteers rather than a company, so it has no headquarters, employee count or commercial funding to report.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: A webadmin interface and command-line tool let users manage the server, and an application catalog offers pre-packaged software, including blogging platforms, cloud file sync tools like Nextcloud, personal websites, RSS readers and collaborative office suites such as OnlyOffice and Collabora, all installable in a few clicks.

Applications that support user registration can integrate with YunoHost's built-in single sign-on and LDAP user directory, so logging into the YunoHost user portal automatically authenticates a user across every connected app.

YunoHost also manages domain, website and email configuration, handling tasks like DNS, TLS certificates and mail delivery that are typically among the most error-prone parts of running a self-hosted server manually.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: YunoHost itself is completely free and open source, with no license fee, subscription or paid tier of any kind.
The only expense involved is whatever hardware or hosting a user chooses to run it on, ranging from a Raspberry Pi or repurposed old computer to a rented virtual private server.
Because there is no commercial entity behind YunoHost, there is also no paid support plan; help comes from community documentation and the volunteer-run project forum.

features.title: One-click application catalog
features.description: Installs pre-packaged applications like Nextcloud, blogs, RSS readers and websites through the webadmin without manual configuration.

features.title: Built-in single sign-on and LDAP
features.description: Lets supported apps authenticate automatically against YunoHost's central user directory once a user logs into the user portal.

features.title: Webadmin and command-line management
features.description: Provides both a graphical web interface and a yunohost command-line tool for server administration.

features.title: Automated domain and TLS management
features.description: Handles DNS, TLS certificate issuance and website configuration to reduce common self-hosting setup errors.

features.title: Email server management
features.description: Provides tools to configure and run a personal or small-organization email service on the same server.

features.title: Backup and restore support
features.description: Applications integrated with YunoHost support standardized backup and restore operations through the platform.

features.title: Collaborative office suite integration
features.description: Supports OnlyOffice and Collabora integration with Nextcloud for in-browser document editing on a self-hosted stack.

features.title: Debian-based, community-maintained core
features.description: Built on Debian GNU/Linux and maintained entirely by volunteer contributors as copylefted libre software.

pros: Completely free and open source with no subscription cost beyond the hardware or hosting a user provides
One-click app catalog substantially lowers the barrier to self-hosting common services compared to manual Linux setup
Built-in SSO and LDAP integration means users do not need separate logins for every self-hosted app
Automated domain, TLS and email configuration removes some of the most error-prone parts of running a server
Community-driven and ethically focused, appealing to privacy-conscious users who want to avoid third-party SaaS platforms
cons: As a volunteer-maintained project, there is no paid support option or guaranteed response time for issues
Still requires basic comfort with server administration concepts even though YunoHost simplifies the process
Application quality and maintenance depend on individual community packagers, so not every app in the catalog is equally polished
Running your own server means taking on responsibility for uptime, security patching and backups yourself

faqs.question: Is YunoHost free?
faqs.answer: Yes, YunoHost is completely free and open source with no license fee or subscription.

faqs.question: What operating system is YunoHost based on?
faqs.answer: YunoHost is based on Debian GNU/Linux.

faqs.question: Who maintains YunoHost?
faqs.answer: YunoHost is maintained entirely by volunteers as a copylefted libre software project; it is not a company or for-profit entity.

faqs.question: Can YunoHost run Nextcloud?
faqs.answer: Yes, Nextcloud is one of the applications available in YunoHost's application catalog and can be installed in a few clicks.

faqs.question: Does YunoHost support single sign-on across apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, applications that support user registration can integrate with YunoHost's built-in LDAP and single sign-on, so one login works across connected apps.

faqs.question: Can YunoHost handle email hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, YunoHost includes tools to configure and manage a personal or small-organization email service on the same server.

faqs.question: When was YunoHost created?
faqs.answer: The project originated in February 2012.

faqs.question: What hardware can I run YunoHost on?
faqs.answer: YunoHost can run on a range of hardware, from a Raspberry Pi or repurposed old computer to a rented virtual private server.

use_cases.title: Personal cloud and file sync
use_cases.description: Individuals self-host Nextcloud through YunoHost to sync and store files privately instead of using third-party cloud storage.

use_cases.title: Small community or organization services
use_cases.description: Small groups run shared blogs, wikis and email on a single YunoHost server with unified login for members.

use_cases.title: Home-lab and self-hosting learning
use_cases.description: Hobbyists use YunoHost on a Raspberry Pi or old computer to learn self-hosting without manually configuring every service.
